summary.intent: Fix multiple issues in the Gossamer VS Code extension: server port conflicts on reload, missing database schema on startup, checkpoints not showing in treeview, and multi-repo session/DB isolation.
summary.outcome: Fixed all identified issues: orphaned server detection/kill, pushSchema bug (silently discarding all SQL statements), per-repo DB registry replacing a single primary DB, gitOid mapping failures due to maxBuffer overflow, and workspace-aware session sorting with localPath propagation throughout the API.

repo: Each repo has its own SQLite DB with no hierarchy — there is no 'primary' DB concept; the server must aggregate across all repo DBs.

The pushSchema function split schema.sql on ';\n' and then filtered chunks starting with '--', but every chunk in schema.sql starts with a '-- CreateTable' comment, silently discarding all 56 CREATE TABLE statements.

The git OID mapping (saveGitOidMapping) was silently failing because gitlog defaults to nameStatus:true (--name-status), causing maxBuffer overflow on large repos; errors were swallowed by a bare catch.
Checkpoint commits live on entire/checkpoints/v1 branch but also have Entire-Checkpoint: trailers on feature branches; findCommitForCheckpoint searches feature branches correctly.
The worktree add command for checkpoints must use --detach to avoid 'branch already used by worktree' errors when multiple repos share the same entire/checkpoints/v1 branch.
macOS com.apple.provenance extended attribute on .db files causes SQLite disk I/O errors; getDb caches the broken Prisma client, requiring evictDb + file deletion + retry.
The dbRegistry (Map<dbPath, PrismaClient>) pattern with allDbs(), dbForRepo(), and resolveDb() probe is the correct architecture for multi-repo support.
resolveDb probes all DBs when no localPath is provided, but the /api/sessions/:id probe was too narrow (only checked shadowSession, missing checkpointSessionMetadata and logEvent tables).
The git fs.watch fires on loose ref file changes under .git/refs/heads/entire/; packed refs would not trigger it.
Live session updates depend entirely on the Entire CLI writing new shadow branch commits; Gossamer's watcher/indexer/broadcast chain works correctly.

code.finding: pushSchema was only called for the primary DB passed via CLI args, not for all configured repos — secondary repo DBs never got schema applied at startup.
httpServer.listen(port) had no EADDRINUSE error handler, causing silent crashes when port was already in use.
spawnServer guard only checked in-memory GossamerPanel.server static variable, not the actual port — orphaned processes from crashed sessions caused EADDRINUSE.
saveGitOidMapping catch block swallows ALL errors silently, including maxBuffer overflow from gitlog.
saveGitOidMapping called with meta.branch from root metadata.json; only skipped if already in mappedCheckpointIds set.
findCommitForCheckpoint was using gitlog with default nameStatus:true, causing maxBuffer exceeded on repos with 10k commits; fix: nameStatus:false + maxBuffer:100MB.
No API calls passed localPath, so all session-specific endpoints fell back to probing all DBs; now passes window.__GOSSAMER_REPO_PATH__ injected per workspace.

workflow: When a fix 'works' in isolation (node --input-type=module test) but fails in production, check compiled dist/ output and run tests against the actual dist/server.js.
Use lsof to find and kill processes holding a port before respawning; adopted servers are often stale/serving wrong repo.
Verify SQLite DB table existence with sqlite3 .tables before concluding pushSchema worked.
Test gitlog output size with raw git log commands before assuming the npm package works — maxBuffer is a common silent failure mode.
fs.watch on .git/refs/ works for loose refs but would miss packed refs; Entire CLI uses loose refs so this is fine for now.

friction: pushSchema silently discarded all 56 SQL statements for multiple sessions before root cause was found — the comment filter bug was non-obvious.
macOS com.apple.provenance xattr caused SQLite disk I/O errors that only appeared for the gossamer-test DB, not the primary one, making it hard to reproduce.
getDb caches broken Prisma clients, requiring evictDb export from core/db.ts — cache eviction wasn't originally designed in.
gitlog maxBuffer overflow was caught by a bare catch in saveGitOidMapping, resulting in 0 git OID mappings with no error logged anywhere.
The resolveDb probe for /api/sessions/:id only checked shadowSession, causing sessions that exist only in checkpointSessionMetadata to return 404.
The 'primary DB' concept was baked deeply into server.ts with the bare `db` variable used in ~15+ endpoints, requiring systematic replacement.
Resumed sessions not showing new messages turned out to be an Entire CLI issue (not writing new shadow branch commits), not a Gossamer bug — significant investigation time spent on a non-issue.
open_items: Live session updates for cosmos-graphql don't work because Entire CLI isn't writing new shadow branch commits when sessions are resumed — needs investigation on the Entire CLI side.
resolveDb probes all DBs sequentially when no localPath is provided — could be slow with many repos; consider adding session-to-repo index.
The restart method in GossamerPanel doesn't wait for the old server to fully die before spawning a new one — potential race condition.
Only 9 of 17 possible git OID mappings were created for cosmos-graphql even after the maxBuffer fix — some commits may be beyond the 10,000 limit or on branches not searched.
The /api/repos/current endpoint was fixed to accept ?localPath= but ensureRepoRegistered behavior when repo is already registered (duplicate POST) should be verified as idempotent.
Sessions endpoint aggregates across all DBs but has no pagination — could be slow with many repos and sessions.
The checkpoint worktree uses --detach which is correct, but stale worktrees from previous sessions may need cleanup on startup.
